title: Laboratory Test Results PDF Extraction: Convert Lab Reports to Excel
metaTitle: Lab Test Results PDF Extraction - Convert to Excel | PDFExcel.ai
metaDescription: Extract structured data from laboratory test results PDFs. Convert lab reports to Excel spreadsheets with AI-powered field extraction.
h1: Laboratory Test Results PDF Extraction Guide

hero.headline: Extract Data from Laboratory Test Results PDFs
hero.subheadline: Convert lab reports and test results into structured Excel spreadsheets for analysis, research, and record management

summary: Laboratory test results PDF extraction involves converting medical lab reports, diagnostic test results, and research data from PDF format into structured Excel spreadsheets. This process transforms unstructured lab data into organized rows and columns for easier analysis, patient tracking, research compilation, and regulatory reporting.
whoItsFor: Healthcare administrators managing patient test records
Medical researchers compiling study data from multiple labs
Laboratory information system managers digitizing historical records
whenThisIsRelevant: Converting paper-based lab reports to digital format
Aggregating test results from multiple laboratory sources
Preparing lab data for statistical analysis or research studies
supportedInputs: Digital PDF lab reports with typed test values
Photo captures of printed test results
expectedOutputs: Excel files with test names, values, and reference ranges in separate columns
CSV data with patient identifiers and corresponding lab values
commonChallenges: Test values scattered across different PDF layouts and formats
Reference ranges mixed with actual results requiring separation
Multi-page reports with patient info on different pages than results
Varying laboratory formats making standardization difficult
howItWorksSteps: Upload your laboratory test results PDF files or images
AI identifies and extracts test names, values, units, and reference ranges
Data is organized into structured columns with one test per row
Download the Excel file with cleanly separated lab data fields
whyPdfExcelAiFits: AI recognizes common lab terminology and medical abbreviations
Handles various laboratory report formats from different providers
Separates numerical values from units and reference ranges automatically
Processes multiple test result pages in batch for efficiency
limitations: Handwritten test values may not extract as accurately as typed results
Complex nested tables with sub-tests may require manual verification
Heavily redacted patient information could affect data extraction completeness

faq.question: Can it extract specific lab values like CBC or chemistry panels?
faq.answer: Yes, the AI recognizes common lab test names and can extract values for blood counts, metabolic panels, lipid profiles, and other standard laboratory tests into separate columns.

faq.question: How does it handle reference ranges and normal values?
faq.answer: The system identifies and separates reference ranges from actual test values, placing them in distinct columns so you can easily compare results against normal ranges.

faq.question: What happens with multi-page lab reports?
faq.answer: Multi-page reports are processed as single documents, with patient information and all test results compiled into one comprehensive Excel row per patient or test date.

faq.question: Can it process lab reports from different laboratories?
faq.answer: Yes, the AI adapts to various laboratory formats and layouts, though some manual field customization may be needed for non-standard report designs from specialized labs.

trustSignals: Files encrypted and automatically deleted after processing
OCR technology handles both digital and scanned lab reports
Pay-as-you-go pricing with free trial available
exampleUseCases: Hospital converting archived paper lab reports to digital format
Research team aggregating blood work results from multiple study participants
Medical practice preparing lab data for insurance reporting
Clinical trial coordinator organizing baseline laboratory values for analysis
